steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection list.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Select an option" dropdown within the Teams Capability popup in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the VS Code window to choose the project root directory.
Execute the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, targeting the workspace "vsacuse_app_ApWgw" shown in the Explorer sidebar.
Type the text ' provision' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box, initiating the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command.
Press the Enter key to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion there's message "needs a Microsoft 365 account" exist.
Click the "Cancel" button in the Microsoft 365 developer sandbox prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface.
